name: The Custom House
description: The most hated building in Boston stands lit against the night sky, its windows yellow with lamplight — inside, Customs Officer Hartwell and his clerks are working late, which is itself unusual enough to draw attention. A British soldier stands at the front door, his bayonet catching the light.
atmosphericDetails: A dispatch rider's horse is tied at the post outside — the animal is lathered, meaning it arrived recently and hard.
Through the ground-floor window, a man in a dark coat can be seen gesturing urgently over a map spread on a table.
A fresh wax seal impression in the mud near the door matches the seal used on official Crown correspondence.
